up.units: grows (sole carbohydrate); higher growth rate/final density than R. gnavus
up.method: OD600 growth curve (Fig. 4c); numeric rate in Supplementary Table 3

growth_records.cite: Lou et al. Infant microbiome cultivation and metagenomic analysis reveal Bifidobacterium 2'-fucosyllactose utilization can be facilitated by coexisting species. Nat Commun (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-43279-y. [PMC10654741]
growth_records.snippet: Both the R. gnavus and B. breve monocultures grew on d-lactose... B. breve had a higher growth rate and reached a higher final cell density than R. gnavus.
growth_records.notes: B. breve grows on D-lactose (positive), higher rate/density than R. gnavus. B. breve did NOT grow on 2'FL or L-fucose (Fig. 4a,b; Supplementary Fig. 7). No numeric rate in fulltext.

up.method: reported specific substrate consumption rate (steady-state substrate balance)
derived: 10 mmol hexose-equiv / 2 (lactose is a disaccharide = 2 hexose)

sec.method: product of bifid shunt; dominant product; time-course concentration only (Fig 3), no specific rate reported
minor product at high growth rate; concentration only (Fig 3)

growth_records.cite: Ortiz Camargo, Angela Rocio et al. Quantitative Physiology and Proteome Adaptations of Bifidobacterium breve NRBB57 at Near-Zero Growth Rates. Microbiology Spectrum (2023). DOI:10.1128/spectrum.02568-22. [PMC10269484]
growth_records.snippet: Even at the highest growth of 0.4 h-1 with a specific substrate consumption rate of 10 mmol hexose equivalents gDW-1 h-1, lactate production was low... lactose was still mainly converted into acetate, formate, and ethanol at high growth rat
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.4 h-1 (growth realized experimentally via CDW measurement; mu=D at steady state). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.4 = 1.733 h. Reported qS = 10 mmol hexose-equiv/gDW/h; lactose is a disaccharide (2 hexose units) so lactose uptake = 10/2 = 5 mmol/gDW/h. Feed lactose = 15 mmol/kg (5.4 g/kg lactose.H2O, MW 360.3 -> 0.01499 mol/kg = 15 mmol/kg, matches paper). Product-specific secretion rates NOT reported numerically in text; only time-course extracellular concentrations shown in Fig 3, so rate set null. Yx/ATP max and m_ATP are pooled chemostat estimates (D=0.025-0.4).

sec.method: dominant bifid-shunt product; no specific rate reported

growth_records.cite: Ortiz Camargo, Angela Rocio et al. Quantitative Physiology and Proteome Adaptations of Bifidobacterium breve NRBB57 at Near-Zero Growth Rates. Microbiology Spectrum (2023). DOI:10.1128/spectrum.02568-22. [PMC10269484]
growth_records.snippet: B. breve NRBB57 was grown in chemostats in biological triplicates at dilution rates of 0.12, 0.25, and 0.4 h-1 in 0.5-L bioreactors and 0.025 h-1 in 1-L bioreactors
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state mu = D = 0.25 h-1. td = ln2/0.25 = 2.773 h. No condition-specific qS or product rate reported for this dilution rate; lactose mainly converted to acetate, formate, ethanol across all chemostat rates. Medium composition full recipe same as D=0.4 record (abbreviated here).

sec.method: dominant bifid-shunt product; no specific rate reported

growth_records.cite: Ortiz Camargo, Angela Rocio et al. Quantitative Physiology and Proteome Adaptations of Bifidobacterium breve NRBB57 at Near-Zero Growth Rates. Microbiology Spectrum (2023). DOI:10.1128/spectrum.02568-22. [PMC10269484]
growth_records.snippet: grown in chemostats in biological triplicates at dilution rates of 0.12, 0.25, and 0.4 h-1 in 0.5-L bioreactors and 0.025 h-1 in 1-L bioreactors
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state mu = D = 0.12 h-1. td = ln2/0.12 = 5.776 h. No condition-specific qS/product rate reported.

sec.method: dominant bifid-shunt product; no specific rate reported

growth_records.cite: Ortiz Camargo, Angela Rocio et al. Quantitative Physiology and Proteome Adaptations of Bifidobacterium breve NRBB57 at Near-Zero Growth Rates. Microbiology Spectrum (2023). DOI:10.1128/spectrum.02568-22. [PMC10269484]
growth_records.snippet: 0.025 h-1 in 1-L bioreactors ... After the chemostat reached the steady state, a polyethersulfone crossflow filter ... was connected ... to begin the retentostat mode
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state mu = D = 0.025 h-1 (lowest dilution rate; 1-L bioreactor). td = ln2/0.025 = 27.72 h. Reference (t=0) state for the retentostat. No condition-specific qS/product rate reported.

up.method: growth-limiting substrate; consumed but no endpoint specific rate reported
consumed as alternative energy source after ~1 wk (Fig 3B); concentration only
consumed ~0.1 mM over run (Fig 4); concentration only
consumed ~0.75 mM over run (UPLC could not separate the two); concentration only

sec.method: increased at near-zero growth (still lowest concentration product)
increased ~2 mM over run, partly from aspartate catabolism (Fig 3B/4)
increased ~0.15 mM (end product of arginine catabolism) (Fig 4)

yields.m_ATP_retentostat_endpoint: 0.915 +/- 0.016 mmol ATP/gDW/h (3.6-fold reduced vs chemostat m_ATP 3.32)

growth_records.cite: Ortiz Camargo, Angela Rocio et al. Quantitative Physiology and Proteome Adaptations of Bifidobacterium breve NRBB57 at Near-Zero Growth Rates. Microbiology Spectrum (2023). DOI:10.1128/spectrum.02568-22. [PMC10269484]
growth_records.snippet: the growth rate progressively reduced to 0.00092 h-1, leading to a 3.4-fold reduction of the maintenance energy requirement ... the latter corresponding to a doubling time of 31 days
growth_records.notes: Retentostat endpoint (~3 wk) growth rate mu = 0.00092 +/- 0.00006 h-1 (model-estimated via modified Verseveld equation; classed calculated). td = ln2/0.00092 = 753.4 h = 31.4 d (paper states ~31 days). Intermediate model-estimated growth rates: week1 = 0.0055 +/- 0.0011, week2 = 0.0018 +/- 0.00005 h-1 (not emitted as separate records; same continuous cultivation). Maintenance m_ATP reduced 3.6-fold from 3.32 to 0.915 mmol ATP/gDW/h. All uptake/secretion listed are experimentally OBSERVED consumptions/productions but reported only as time-course concentrations (delta-mM given for aspartate 0.1, gln/arg 0.75, succinate 2, ornithine 0.15, NH3 1) with no biomass-normalized specific rates, hence rate=null. Amino acids alanine, asparagine, cystine, glutamate, glycine, histidine, isoleucine, leucine, lysine, methionine, phenylalanine, proline, threonine, tryptophan, tyrosine, valine did NOT decrease (not utilized).

growth_records.medium: GITS dilution medium + GOS-FOS-XOS prebiotics (total 10 g/L carbohydrate)
growth_records.cite: Adamberg et al. Survival and synergistic growth of mixed cultures of bifidobacteria and lactobacilli combined with prebiotic oligosaccharides in a gastrointestinal tract simulator. Microbial Ecology in Health and Disease (2014). DOI:10.3402/mehd.v25.23062. [PMC4101457]
growth_records.snippet: B. breve 46 had the highest average specific growth rate on both substrate combinations (muave=0.43+/-0.09 and 0.33+/-0.03 h-1, in GOS-FOS-XOS and GOS-SS media, respectively)
growth_records.notes: Per-strain specific growth rate reported in Table 4 (GOS-FOS-XOS medium): mu=0.43+/-0.09 /h. Authors computed mu(strain)=D+d(N)/(dt*N) from cfu/mL counts and dilution rate during 6.5-24 h dilution phase. doubling_time=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.43=1.61 h. Consumption/secretion are co-culture-level (not strain-resolved), captured in the separate co-culture aggregate record for this medium. Total carbohydrate 10 g/L, ~5% (3.2 mM) as free mono/disaccharides.

growth_records.medium: GITS dilution medium + GOS + soluble starch (total 10 g/L carbohydrate)
growth_records.cite: Adamberg et al. Survival and synergistic growth of mixed cultures of bifidobacteria and lactobacilli combined with prebiotic oligosaccharides in a gastrointestinal tract simulator. Microbial Ecology in Health and Disease (2014). DOI:10.3402/mehd.v25.23062. [PMC4101457]
growth_records.snippet: muave=0.43+/-0.09 and 0.33+/-0.03 h-1, in GOS-FOS-XOS and GOS-SS media, respectively
growth_records.notes: Table 4 GOS-SS medium: mu(B. breve 46)=0.33+/-0.03 /h via mu=D+d(N)/(dt*N). doubling_time=0.6931/0.33=2.10 h. Products are co-culture aggregate, in the separate aggregate record for GOS-SS.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 1696 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/1696

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 1696 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/1696

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 1696 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/1696
